bread_ration.name: bread ration
bread_ration.lore: A dense loaf of twice-baked trail bread, hard enough to crack a tooth but sustaining enough to keep a dungeon delver moving for hours. Adventurers claim the dusty flavor grows on you — they are lying.

dried_meat.name: dried meat
dried_meat.lore: Salt-cured strips of some unidentified creature, wound tight and wrapped in waxed cloth. The jerky is chewy and intensely flavored — not pleasant, but undeniably filling and easy to carry.

hard_cheese.name: hard cheese
hard_cheese.lore: A wheel of cave-aged hard cheese, its rind thick and its interior dense as packed earth. Dungeon merchants prize it because it keeps indefinitely in cool passages and provides solid sustenance without the mess of liquids.

wild_mushroom.name: wild mushroom
wild_mushroom.lore: A large cap of unknown subterranean mushroom, faintly luminescent at the gills. Consuming it restores stamina and has mild restorative properties — dungeon foragers claim certain varieties even close minor wounds.

apple.name: apple
apple.lore: A crisp, red apple — impossibly fresh considering the depth at which it was found. The flesh is cool and slightly sweet, and eating it leaves the body feeling subtly mended, as though the fruit itself knows what healing means.

trail_rations.name: trail rations
trail_rations.lore: A substantial pack of mixed provisions — dried fruit, salted crackers, hard tack, and a small sealed jar of rendered fat. It's not glamorous but it's thorough, and experienced delvers always carry a week's worth.

healing_herb.name: healing herb
healing_herb.lore: A bundle of bruised leaves that smell faintly of pine and citrus. When consumed, they release compounds that accelerate blood clotting and reduce inflammation — healers once grew these in monastery gardens, but wild specimens thrive in dungeon crevices.

elvish_bread.name: elvish bread
elvish_bread.lore: Baked under starlight using grain that grows only in enchanted groves, this pale, dense loaf is infused with vitality that sustains the body long after ordinary food would be exhausted. A single piece keeps travelers going for days in the world above — down here, its power is more modest but still remarkable.

hearty_stew.name: hearty stew
hearty_stew.lore: A thick, rich stew of root vegetables and tender meat, still warm in its sealed clay pot. The recipe, passed down through generations of dungeon tavern-keepers, has been refined to maximize the fighting edge it gives to anyone who eats it — warriors speak of it almost reverently.

deep_fungi.name: deep fungi
deep_fungi.lore: A cluster of deep-dwelling mushrooms that grow only in the lowest passages where no light has ever reached. They absorb residual magical energy from the stone itself, concentrating it in their flesh. Eating them is intensely disorienting for a moment, then extraordinarily nourishing — the body adapts to the depth they came from.

void_ration.name: void ration
void_ration.lore: A dense brick of compressed sustenance infused with void essence, found only in the abyssal depths where conventional food would rot instantly. Consuming it fills the body with a cold, focused energy that sharpens combat reflexes to an inhuman degree. Those who eat it report that hunger becomes an abstract concept for hours afterward.

philosopher_tea.name: philosopher's tea
philosopher_tea.lore: Brewed from leaves found only near ancient ruins, this deep violet tea clears the mind and sharpens perception in ways ordinary stimulants cannot approach. Scholars who have tasted it describe a heightened awareness that makes quiz questions seem to answer themselves.

ambrosia.name: Ambrosia
ambrosia.lore: The food of the Olympian gods. Homer uses the word interchangeably with nectar — sometimes it is food, sometimes drink, sometimes an anointing oil rubbed on the dead to preserve them. Pindar says it conferred immortality. What is certain is that mortals who tasted it were never the same. It restores everything and improves everything — but it is not meant for mortal tongues.

golden_apple.name: Golden Apple of Hesperides
golden_apple.lore: Heracles's eleventh labor sent him to the Garden of the Hesperides at the western edge of the world, guarded by the hundred-headed dragon Ladon, to retrieve the golden apples that Gaia had given Hera as a wedding gift. The apples granted immortality to whoever ate them. Eris later used one inscribed 'For the Fairest' to start the Trojan War. Beauty, immortality, and war — all in one fruit.

peach_of_immortality.name: Peach of Immortality
peach_of_immortality.lore: Xi Wangmu, the Queen Mother of the West, grows these peaches in her garden on Mount Kunlun. They ripen once every three thousand years. Sun Wukong ate them all during his rampage through Heaven, gaining near-immortality. Journey to the West records that the theft of the peaches was one of three offenses that caused Buddha to imprison the Monkey King beneath Five Elements Mountain for five hundred years.

tantalus_plum.name: Tantalus's Plum
tantalus_plum.unidentified_name: a perfect-looking fruit just out of reach
tantalus_plum.lore: Tantalus served his own son Pelops as a feast for the gods, testing their omniscience. His punishment in Tartarus was eternal hunger and thirst: water receded when he bent to drink, and fruit branches pulled away when he reached. This plum is the fruit he could never grasp — eating it heals greatly but leaves you ravenous.

magic_dungeon_carrot.name: Magic Dungeon Carrot
magic_dungeon_carrot.lore: A luminous orange carrot that grows only in the enchanted soil of deep dungeon gardens. It glows faintly with an inner light and smells of wildflowers. Legend says it is the favorite food of a certain magical beast found deeper in the dungeon. You could eat it... but perhaps it would be wiser to hold onto it.
